steps.name: Violation: color-contrast - Elements must meet minimum color contrast ratio thresholds

statusDetails.message:
IMPACT: This issue significantly affects users with visual impairments relying on screen readers. It prevents them from understanding the purpose of the color-contrast element.

FIX: To fix this, add a descriptive 'aria-label' or 'alt' attribute to the element. Example: <element aria-label='Elements must meet minimum color contrast ratio thresholds'>...</element>
statusDetails.trace:
Description: Ensures the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds
Nodes: 5

steps.name: Violation: heading-order - Heading levels should only increase by one

statusDetails.message:
IMPACT: This issue significantly affects users with visual impairments relying on screen readers. It prevents them from understanding the purpose of the heading-order element.

FIX: To fix this, add a descriptive 'aria-label' or 'alt' attribute to the element. Example: <element aria-label='Heading levels should only increase by one'>...</element>
statusDetails.trace:
Description: Ensures the order of headings is semantically correct
Nodes: 9

steps.name: Violation: region - All page content should be contained by landmarks

statusDetails.message:
IMPACT: This issue significantly affects users with visual impairments relying on screen readers. It prevents them from understanding the purpose of the region element.

FIX: To fix this, add a descriptive 'aria-label' or 'alt' attribute to the element. Example: <element aria-label='All page content should be contained by landmarks'>...</element>
statusDetails.trace:
Description: Ensures all page content is contained by landmarks
